payload.answer: This appears to be a duplicate. The existing memory says “Sam from Goldman mentioned that he likes Bill Evans,” and the new memory says “Sam from Goldman said he likes Bill Evans.” These express the same durable fact: Sam from Goldman likes Bill Evans. Safe actions: link_duplicate to connect the new item to the existing memory as the same fact, keep_existing to retain only the current memory, add_anyway if you intentionally want both versions stored, edit to revise the wording before saving, or cancel to take no action.
citations: Existing memory: Sam from Goldman mentioned that he likes Bill Evans
New memory: Sam from Goldman said he likes Bill Evans

entity_resolution.reason: The new memory has the same meaning as the existing memory; the difference is only wording/punctuation-level phrasing.
